t_ch: 可实际上呢？与其说是心地善良，倒不如说是自私伪善；与其说是细腻敏锐，倒不如说是胆小怕事。至于认真负责？
t_en: But in practice? When they say "kind-hearted," they actually mean selfish and hypocritical. When they say "meticulous and perceptive," they actually mean cowardly and timid. And as for "responsible"?

t_ch: 哈哈，看看我们可怜的M吧——就是因为盲目轻信了那位阿然女士，才会被人不知用什么手段，险些弄成精神残废——这就是那所谓的鼓励师带给我们的「安慰与治愈」！
t_en: Ha, just look at poor old M — he was gullible and ended up nearly mentally damaged by that Miss Ran and some unknown tricks of hers. That's all the "comfort and healing" that so-called Motivators brought us!

t_ch: W望着你，笑意盈盈，却仿佛下一秒就会跳起来咬断谁的脖子。
t_en: W looks at you with a beaming smile on his face, but he still seems as though he's about to jump up and bite someone right in the neck.

t_ch: 嗯？还有什么事吗？想和我这个「疯子」把酒言欢？
t_en: Well? Anything else? Would you like to drink, chat, and be merry with a "madman" like me?

t_ch: 唔…我倒是不介意。你真是个怪胎，哈哈哈！不过，我喜欢你——要是M能有你一半有趣就好了！
t_en: Oh... I don't mind. You really are a freak, y'know? Haha! But I like you — if only M were half as fun as you!

t_ch: 「砰」——W跳上了桌台，居高临下地看着你。
t_en: *thud* W jumps up onto the table and, having taken the high ground, looks down his nose at you.

t_ch: 你知道吗，别看那家伙平时装得一本正经、人畜无害，实际上可比我糟糕多了，他只是喜欢扮演受害者罢了——
t_en: You know what? Even though that guy pretends to be all serious like he wouldn't harm a fly, in actuality he's far worse than me. He just likes to play the victim...

t_ch: 摆出一副软弱可怜的样子，然后在你渐渐放下戒心后，不遗余力地从你身上榨取关怀与关注，简直比缠在树上的藤还要麻烦。
t_en: Wearing this weak, pathetic mask and then — once you've let down your guard — doing everything possible to squeeze all the attention and care from you. It's worse than a thorny ivy coiling around a tree.

t_ch: 即便M是真心想要个朋友…可就他那种烂到骨子里的性格，除了我还有谁受得了他呢？
t_en: Even if M really does just want a friend... who out there besides me is going to put up with that trash personality of his?

t_ch: 不过你也用不着可怜他。他早该明白，像我们这种怪胎，怎么可能会讨人喜欢？都这样了还不远万里跑来玩什么找朋友游戏，真不知道是倔还是傻……
t_en: But anyway, you don't need to feel sorry for him. He should've figured out a long time ago that freaks like us... we can never be lovable. It's just the way it is, so what's the point in journeying all this way to play at making friends? I honestly can't tell if it's stubbornness or foolishness...

t_ch: 说起来，你看起来也是M会喜欢的类型。可千万小心别被那家伙缠上，哈哈。
t_en: Speaking of which, you seem just like M's type. Just be careful not to get tangled up with that guy, haha.

t_ch: 你看这人怎么回事？
t_en: What's up with this guy?

t_ch: 看着就吓人，我们离他远点…
t_en: He looks freaky. Let's get away from him...

t_ch: 我倒是还挺喜欢你的，不过…你看起来也是M会喜欢的类型。
t_en: I actually quite like you, but... you also seem to be just like M's type.

t_ch: 可千万小心别被那家伙缠上，哈哈。
t_en: Be careful not to get tangled up with that guy, haha.

t_ch: 皮皮西安静地望向你，神色柔和——你知道那是M。
t_en: The Pepeshi watches you quietly, with a gentle look — you know that is M.

t_ch: …我…但愿我…之前的某些…言行，并没有为您带来太多麻烦……
t_en: I... I hope my... words before... didn't cause you any trouble...

t_ch: 抱歉！我已经很努力地在控制自己了…但……
t_en: Sorry! I've been trying very hard to control myself... but...

t_ch: 总、总之，希望我能对此做出一些微不足道的补偿。若是不嫌弃，这个还请您收下吧。
t_en: A—Anyway, I hope I can make up for this in a small way. If you don't mind, please, take this.

t_ch: …我…但愿我…之前的某些…言行，并没有为您带来太多麻烦……
t_en: I... I hope my... words before... didn't cause you any trouble...

t_ch: 抱歉！我已经很努力地在控制自己了…但……
t_en: Sorry! I've been trying very hard to control myself... but...

t_ch: 总、总之，希望我能对此做出一些微不足道的补偿。
t_en: A—Anyway, I hope I can make up for this in a small way.

t_ch: 眼前的皮皮西看起来像是一只带着裂痕的陶瓷茶杯，轻触即碎。
t_en: The Pepeshi looks like a cracked porcelain teacup that would shatter at the faintest touch.

t_ch: 您曾感到孤独吗？不瞒您说，这种情绪就像是一道固执的影子，自童年起便终日在我身边萦绕不去。
t_en: Have you ever felt lonely? To be honest, this feeling has been like a shadow lingering over me since childhood that has refused to go away.

t_ch: 亲情、友情、爱情，这些我统统没有。生活像个大泥潭，时刻也不忘将我拖进深处。
t_en: Family, friends, and lovers — I don't have any of those. Life is like living in quicksand, always being dragged down into those depths.

t_ch: 那个…W…他是我的梦魇，但也是我的稻草。他既极端又强势，但也是我唯一的陪伴与救赎。我讨厌他，又离不开他…我们相互支撑，又相互伤害。
t_en: And W... He's my nightmare, but also my last hope. He is extreme and strong, but also my only companion and salvation. I hate him, but could never leave him... We support each other, but we also hurt each other.

t_ch: 我也曾挣扎过，一次又一次，希望能与他人建立亲密关系…可到头来，最后还是只剩我们俩。
t_en: I've struggled time and again before, hoping to build an intimate connection with someone... but in the end, it's still just us two.

t_ch: 我以为所谓的「梦想之城」能够令人梦想成真，以为在梦里肯定有人能包容我的缺陷，可结果……
t_en: I thought the so-called "City of Dreams" could make people's dreams come true, and that there'd be someone in a dream who could put up with all my flaws. But alas...

t_ch: 我好像…陷得更深了，稍微有点透不过气。真希望有谁能来拉我一把……
t_en: It seems like... my flaws are getting worse. I feel like I'm suffocating. I really hope someone can give me a hand...

t_ch: 皮皮西专注地望着你，好似你就是他整个世界的中心。
t_en: The Pepeshi stares intently at you, as if you were at the very center of his universe.

t_ch: 无论如何，我都该好好感谢您才行…不单单是因为您救了我，更因为您对我…对我们的包容和理解。
t_en: In any case, I should be thanking you... Not just for saving me, but also... because of your understanding and putting up with me.

t_ch: 这里是一点微不足道的谢礼，聊表心意，请您务必收下。
t_en: This is a tiny gift just to say thanks and show my appreciation. Please, take it.

t_ch: 无论如何，我都该好好感谢您才行…不单单是因为您救了我，更因为您对我…对我们的包容和理解。
t_en: In any case, I should be thanking you... Not just for saving me, but also... because of your understanding and putting up with me.

t_ch: 有件事我犹豫很久了…恕我冒昧，但我是否有幸与您成为朋友呢？
t_en: There's something I've been thinking about for a long time... Pardon me for being so forward, but would I be able to consider you a friend?

t_ch: 说来可能有些奇怪，但面对您时，我竟生出一种能够被倾听、被理解的幸福感。这种感觉就像苏乐达糖浆一样让人欲罢不能。
t_en: It might sound strange, but when I converse with you, I feel a happiness that comes from being listened to and being understood. This happiness is as addicting as SoulGlad.

t_ch: 抱歉，我知道这种唐突的请求有多么失礼，但…请您考虑考虑吧，W也非常喜欢您呢。
t_en: My apologies. I know it is very forward to make such an abrupt request, but... please think about it. W really likes you, too.

t_ch: 救命——
t_en: Help —

t_ch: 您好，请问有什么是我可以为您效劳的吗？
t_en: Hello, is there anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 无论您有怎样的烦恼，都可以向我倾诉。
t_en: Whatever worries you might have, you can tell me all about it.

t_ch: 我会倾听您的心声与诉求，并力所能及地为您提供帮助。
t_en: I will listen to your thoughts and wishes, and provide as much help as I possibly can.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: 哈？就算我对你这家伙印象还不错，再敢乱开这样的玩笑，小心挨揍哦？
t_en: What? Sure, we've been getting on well, but if you dare to joke around like that again, I'll give you a hiding, okay?

t_ch: 不过嘛…你要是实在想自找麻烦，倒是可以问问M——当然，如果你不怕之后被他像狗皮膏药一样缠上的话！
t_en: But... if you really want to get yourself in a pickle, you can always ask M — of course, only if you don't mind him sticking to you like a piece of chewing gum in the future!

t_ch: 您好，请问有什么是我可以为您效劳的吗？
t_en: Hello, is there anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 我是梦境鼓励师阿然，无论您有怎样的烦恼，都可以向我倾诉。
t_en: I am Ran, a Dreamscape Motivator. Whatever worries you might have, you can tell me all about it.

t_ch: 我会倾听您的心声与诉求，并力所能及地为您提供帮助。
t_en: I will listen to your thoughts and wishes, and provide as much help as I possibly can.

t_ch: 梦境鼓励师是什么？
t_en: What is a Dreamscape Motivator?

t_ch: 梦境鼓励师是一种匹诺康尼本地特有的职业——用通俗一点的话来解释，我们的职责和心理医生有些类似。
t_en: It's a special kind of job unique to Penacony — in layman's terms, our duties are similar to a psychologist's.

t_ch: 我们致力于「倾听」和「疏导」，帮助宾客们排除有可能危害到「美梦」稳定性的负面情绪。
t_en: We are committed to "listening" and "guiding" guests to help them expunge any negative emotions that might endanger the stability of their sweet dreams.

t_ch: 在匹诺康尼，筑梦师们负责铸造梦境世界的基建，为宾客们提供满意的游玩体验；而我们鼓励师则负责保障宾客的精神稳定，确保「美梦」的繁荣能够延续。
t_en: In Penacony, the Dreamweavers are responsible for crafting out the foundations of the Dreamscape and providing guests with a satisfactory entertainment experience. Meanwhile, we Dreamscape Motivators are responsible for maintaining the guests' mental stability, ensuring that the prosperity of the sweet dream can continue to flourish.

t_ch: 在梦境世界里，人们的情绪会被无止境地放大。一些危险的负面情绪如果没有及时得到处理，很可能就会像瘟疫一样扩散，带来巨大的危害。
t_en: In the Dreamscape, people's emotions are amplified endlessly. Dangerous negative emotions can spread like a plague if left untreated, and cause massive harm.

t_ch: 我们的职责就是及时摘除这些祸种，避免它们在宾客间蔓延开来。
t_en: Our job is to remove these blights in a timely manner and prevent them spreading between guests.

t_ch: 那么，您有什么想要咨询的吗？
t_en: So, would you like to consult me about anything?

t_ch: 来聊聊吧。
t_en: Let's have a chat.

t_ch: 这工作听上去很辛苦。
t_en: This sounds like hard work.

t_ch: 是啊…确实很辛苦，但同时也很快乐。
t_en: Yeah... It really is hard work, but it's also lots of fun.

t_ch: 当看到别人因自己的帮助而重新振作起来时，那种满足感是难以言喻的。
t_en: That feeling of satisfaction you get when you see someone pick themselves up again because of your help... it's beyond words.

t_ch: 这也是我选择成为梦境鼓励师的原因。
t_en: That's also why I chose to become a Dreamscape Motivator in the first place.

t_ch: 遇到处理不了的怎么办？
t_en: What if you run into a problem that you can't handle?

t_ch: 的确，我们偶尔会遇上一些比较…极端的求助者。他们可能会表现出明显的偏执或暴力倾向。
t_en: Indeed, we sometimes encounter some more... extreme clients. They might display overt tendencies for obsession or violence.

t_ch: 如果是在现实中，这些人将会是危险的代名词。但这里是匹诺康尼——家族会在保证我们与宾客共同利益的前提下，对事态进行妥善处理。
t_en: In the realm of reality, these people would be the very personification of danger. But we are in Penacony — The Family will handle things appropriately while ensuring the best interests of us and the guests.

t_ch: 就先到这里吧。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 好的，愿您在匹诺康尼度过一段愉快的时光。
t_en: Alright. I hope you have an enjoyable time in Penacony.

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 我平时就在这里当班，如果有需要，您可以随时来找我。
t_en: I'm normally on shift here. Come find me anytime you need something.

t_ch: 阿然专注地望着你，目光沉静、满含鼓励的意味。
t_en: Ran gazes at you intently, her eyes serene and filled with overtones of encouragement.

t_ch: 无论您有怎样的烦恼，都可以向我倾诉。我会倾听您的心声与诉求，并力所能及地为您提供帮助。
t_en: No matter what troubles befall you, you can always tell it to me. I will listen to your thoughts and wishes, and provide as much help as I possibly can.

t_ch: 那么，您想要聊聊吗——
t_en: So, would you like to talk...?

t_ch: 做我们这一行的，一向很少向人抱怨……
t_en: In this line of work, we rarely ever complain to anyone...

t_ch: 我…我已经受够了！有病吃药很难吗？很难吗？！
t_en: I... I've had enough! Is it really so hard to take medicine when you're sick? Is it???

t_ch: 动不动就摆出一副受害者的嘴脸，到处索求关注、博取同情——简直是一群精神蠹虫！
t_en: Always playing the victim at the drop of a hat, milking sympathy and attention all over the place... they're positively a swarm of mental lepisma!

t_ch: 对，鼓励师的确是该心怀善意，为宾客排忧解难…但我们也是人！不是用来盛装情绪垃圾的垃圾桶！
t_en: Yes, Motivators should be kind-hearted and solve the guests' concerns... but we're also people! We're not receptacles for emotional trash!

t_ch: 那些负面情绪飘散在空气里，就像传播瘟病的孢子…而我们既要想尽办法把那些污染源从火坑里拉出来，还要时刻警惕不被他们扯进去。
t_en: Those negative emotions float in the air like spores spreading a plague... We have to both pull those sources of contamination out of the fire, while staying vigilant not to be pulled in by them.

t_ch: 可总有些人…不但自己不愿付出任何努力，还在喋喋不休地埋怨我们不够负责、不够体贴……
t_en: But there are always people... Not only do they refuse to make any effort, they also jabber on complaining about how we aren't responsible or considerate enough...

t_ch: 这种无可救药的家伙…根本连做梦的资格都不配拥有！
t_en: These incorrigible filths... they don't even deserve to dream!

t_ch: 阿然的仪态依旧优雅得体，但你分明窥见了她神情中的麻木与空洞。
t_en: Ran's demeanor is still graceful and elegant, but you can clearly see apathy and emptiness behind those eyes.

t_ch: 嘘…您听到了吗？广场对面有人在哭…有人在笑。哭是因为心有渴望，笑是自嘲潦倒……
t_en: Shh... did you hear that? Someone is crying across the plaza... and someone's laughing. They cry because they still have desires, and they laugh out of ironic satire to their own despondency...

t_ch: 听不到吗？那…您能看到吗？那个人是黛紫色的…这份…嫉妒，因身旁的玫红与明黄而生……
t_en: You can't hear that? Then... do you see that? That person is lilac... This... jealousy... stems from the rose-red and the bright yellow next to them...

t_ch: 啊…您也看不到吗？那您可…真幸福。
t_en: Ah... You can't see these, either? Then you are... truly happy.

t_ch: 那些声音、那些颜色、甚至…我每天都能听到、都能看到。不是此刻这般远远倾听或观望，它们是如此强烈、如此鲜明，气势汹汹地逼近，像洪水一样近乎将我淹没。
t_en: Those voices, those colors, and even... I hear and see them every day. Instead of listening or observing from a distance like we are now, they are intense and brilliant as they aggressively approach, almost drowning me like a torrential flood.

t_ch: 他们的灵魂摊开在我面前…不断拉扯着我的…我因他们的喜怒而喜怒，为他们的哀惧而哀惧。
t_en: Their souls are spread out before me... ceaselessly pulling at me... I am happy because of their joy, and I dread because of their fear.

t_ch: 但我渐渐开始感到…迷惑…就像被巨浪来回拨弄的小船…它们实在太吵闹、太晃眼了…我渐渐开始感受不到自己了……
t_en: But I'm gradually beginning to feel... confused... like a tiny boat being tossed around on gigantic waves... they are simply too noisy, too blinding... I'm starting to lose any sense of self...

t_ch: 我已经快要找不到方向了…已经快要找不到「我」了，我不想再做梦了……
t_en: I'm almost completely lost and disoriented... I can barely find "me" anymore. I don't want to dream anymore...

t_ch: …糖浆呢，我的糖浆呢？哈…哈哈…你需要糖浆吗？
t_en: ...Syrup, where's my syrup? Ha... Haha... Do you need syrup?

t_ch: 快呀——快喝呀！还在等什么呢，快喝呀！
t_en: Quick — drink up! What are you waiting for? Drink up quick!

t_ch: 嘘…您听到了吗？广场对面有人在哭…有人在笑。哭是因为心有渴望，笑是自嘲潦倒……
t_en: Shh... did you hear that? Someone is crying across the plaza... and someone's laughing. They cry because they still have desires, and they laugh out of ironic satire to their own despondency...

t_ch: 听不到吗？那…您能看到吗？那个人是黛紫色的…这份…嫉妒，因身旁的玫红与明黄而生……
t_en: You can't hear that? Then... do you see that? That person is lilac... This... jealousy... stems from the rose-red and the bright yellow next to them...

t_ch: 啊…您也看不到吗？那您可…真幸福。
t_en: Ah... You can't see these, either? Then you are... truly happy.

t_ch: 那些声音、那些颜色、甚至…我每天都能听到、都能看到。不是此刻这般远远倾听或观望，它们是如此强烈、如此鲜明，气势汹汹地逼近，像洪水一样近乎将我淹没。
t_en: Those voices, those colors, and even... I hear and see them every day. Instead of listening or observing from a distance like we are now, they are intense and brilliant as they aggressively approach, almost drowning me like a torrential flood.

t_ch: 他们的灵魂摊开在我面前…不断拉扯着我的…我因他们的喜怒而喜怒，为他们的哀惧而哀惧。
t_en: Their souls are spread out before me... ceaselessly pulling at me... I am happy because of their joy, and I dread because of their fear.

t_ch: 但我渐渐开始感到…迷惑…就像被巨浪来回拨弄的小船…它们实在太吵闹、太晃眼了…我渐渐开始感受不到自己了……
t_en: But I'm gradually beginning to feel... confused... like a tiny boat being tossed around on gigantic waves... they are simply too noisy, too blinding... I'm starting to lose any sense of self...

t_ch: 我已经快要找不到方向了…已经快要找不到「我」了，我不想再做梦了……
t_en: I'm almost completely lost and disoriented... I can barely find "me" anymore. I don't want to dream anymore...

t_ch: 阿然女士身上的沉稳气质稍稍褪去，流露出了些许属于少女的活泼。
t_en: Miss Ran's calm demeanor fades ever-so-slightly, revealing a glimpse of the lively spirit of a young girl.

t_ch: 这「梦想之城」…可真是个好地方啊！它能让人感到自由自在，感到自己充满了…力量。
t_en: This City of Dreams... it really is a wonderful place! It lets you feel completely free, lets you feel like you're full of... power.

t_ch: 该怎么形容呢？哈…在真实的世界中，您若是想要干涉、影响他人，就得小心翼翼地交流、潜移默化地引导。那可是个漫长且枯燥的活计。
t_en: How should I describe it? Ha... If you want to meddle with or influence someone else in the real world, you have to communicate with them ever so carefully, guiding them over time in imperceptible ways. That would a long and arduous job.

t_ch: 但「梦」放大了一切、美化了一切！在这里，我可以更加清晰地「看到」、「听到」、「感知到」，更加轻易地去塑造、去引导那些…迷途的心灵。
t_en: But "dreams" magnify and embellish everything! I can more clearly "see," "hear," and "perceive" in here. I can more easily shape and guide those... lost souls.

t_ch: 它们有的太吵闹、有的很泥泞，有些烈得像火、有些冷得像冰…可只要找对方法，就能让它们变做「理想的样式」、成为「正确的模板」……
t_en: Some are too noisy, while some are muddy. Some are hot as flames, while some are cold as ice... But so long as you can find the right method, you can turn them into "idealized styles" and "correct templates"...

t_ch: 所以…有谁能拒绝呢？谁能拒绝这种难以言喻的…快乐呢？
t_en: So... who can say no to this? Who can resist this inexplicable... joy?

t_ch: 这种感受，像您这样的人…一定可以明白的，对吧？
t_en: People like you can definitely understand this kind of feeling, right?

t_ch: 总感觉怪怪的…有谁在看着我吗？
t_en: Something's off... Is someone watching me?

t_ch: 这应该就是科琳要的东西了…回去找她看看吧。
t_en: This must be what Colleen wanted... Let's head back and see her.

t_ch: 那是…科琳的房间？怎么会有公司员工堵在门口？
t_en: Is that... Colleen's room? Why are there IPC workers blocking the door?

t_ch: …现在的电影业已经堕落成这样了吗？什么滥竽都敢拿来充数——它配吗！
t_en: ...Is this what the movie industry has degraded to? They'll use any old token to fill in the gaps — is that deserved?

t_ch: 你什么你！歌舞片就不要逻辑吗、就不用演技吗？找群小白脸在那乱喊乱叫、蹦蹦跳跳，你管这叫电影？
t_en: "I" what!? Don't musicals need to have logic to them, or acting skills? This is just a bunch of pretty boys yelling and jumping around. You call that a movie?

t_ch: 不、不是……
t_en: N—No...

t_ch: 不是什么不是！我告诉你，这样的垃圾放出来就是在污染环境！你知道一部烂片能对人的身心造成多么严重的危害吗？
t_en: "No" WHAT!? Let me tell you, this kind of trash actually pollutes the environment! Do you know how much harm a bad movie can do to a person's mind and body?

t_ch: 您、您说得有道理…可、可我只是个广告牌啊……
t_en: Y—You make a good point... B—But I'm just a billboard...

t_ch: 广告牌怎么了？广告牌就能随随便便荼毒他人了吗——我告诉你，现在这个破广告给我造成了严重的心理创伤，你打算怎么补偿？
t_en: So what if you're a billboard? Billboards can still deliver wanton suffering to people — let me tell you, this evil ad has brought me severe psychological trauma. How do you plan on making compensation?

t_ch: 我…我不知道…哎呦！好心人帮帮我！
t_en: I—I don't know... Ouch! Please, someone help me!

t_ch: 怎么有人欺负广告牌啊？
t_en: Hey... How could you bully a billboard?

t_ch: 欺负？这年头连说实话都违法了吗？既然它敢放出这样的广告，就早该做好被骂的准备！
t_en: Bullying? Is merely telling the truth enough to get you canceled nowadays? Since it dared to display these ads to the world, it should also be prepared to get chewed out!

t_ch: 再说了，就算真欺负它又如何——怎么着，一个广告牌还能举报我？
t_en: And what's more, even if it really was bullying — so what? Is a billboard capable of reporting me?

t_ch: 掏出手机。
t_en: Pull out your phone.

t_ch: 等等、你等等，现在的年轻人怎么这么开不起玩笑！
t_en: Hang on, wait up. Kids these days don't know how to take a joke!

t_ch: 掏出球棒。
t_en: Pull out a baseball bat.

t_ch: 等等，你要做什么？玩笑都听不懂吗！
t_en: Hang on, what are you going to do with that? Don't you get the joke?

t_ch: 年纪轻轻这么急躁可不好…来来来，先喝瓶苏乐达冷静一下。
t_en: It's no good being so brash at such a young age... Come on, let's drink a bottle of SoulGlad to calm the nerves a little, huh?

t_ch: 所以你欺负广告牌干嘛？
t_en: So why are you bullying the billboard, then?

t_ch: 锐评不能算欺负…锐评！影评人的事，怎么能算欺负？
t_en: Strident criticism does not constitute bullying! Strident criticism, I say! How can a film critic ever be accused of bullying?

t_ch: 我好歹也算个有头有脸的电影评论家，对烂片零容忍怎么了？
t_en: After all, I'm a respected film critic with zero tolerance for bad movies. And what of it?

t_ch: 什么电影把你气成这样？
t_en: What movie made you so angry?

t_ch: 你没看到广告牌上的海报吗？《汽水人生》——最近新上的一部青春歌舞片。
t_en: Didn't you see the poster on the billboard? "Life's a Soda" — the latest hot young musical.

t_ch: 不过我并不推荐你观看。毕竟比起电影，这片子更像是苏乐达公司用钱砸出来的、一部长达两小时的无聊广告。
t_en: But I can't recommend it to you. After all, it's more like a boring two-hour-long commercial that SoulGlad threw money advertising rather than an actual movie.

t_ch: 还什么「梦境匹诺康尼，真正的繁星所在」…呸！要我看啊，这个希米亚影业，根本就是个财大气粗却毫无内涵的罐头工厂。
t_en: And what about "The Penacony Dreamscape, where the real stars orbit"... Ptooey! This Himemia Films is nothing more than a packing house for big budget blockbusters devoid of any real meaning.

t_ch: 你看看他们最近新上的片子，都是个什么玩意——
t_en: Just look at their latest crop of movies. What are they all about...

t_ch: 《重返梦境》？哼，宣发倒是做足了架子，可惜从头到尾都是花架子！
t_en: Return to Dreamscape? Hmph, marketing did a great job trying to stand this up, but it's all vacuous from beginning to end!

t_ch: 《审判之日》？就这叙事、这运镜，还百亿级知名导演，我看是知名现眼！
t_en: Judgment Day? With this description, this camerawork, and the ten-billion-dollar director attached, I'm sure it will be a famous source of embarrassment!

t_ch: 还有这个《迷因危机》！多少年前的故事了，还敢拿出来炒冷饭？
t_en: And this one, Meme Crisis! How stale is that old story? Yet they're still trying to warm it up like leftovers?

t_ch: 哼，粗糙干瘪、难以下咽，还有一股铜臭味……
t_en: Hmph, rough cuts and bone dry, very hard to digest. Smells just like a cash grab to me...

t_ch: 你是影评家？
t_en: Are you a film critic?

t_ch: 没错！匹诺康尼著名影评杂志——《银幕冲击波》，听过吗？没听过就是你孤陋寡闻。
t_en: That's right! Ever heard of the famous Penacony film mag, Silver Screen Shockwave? If not, you must be ill-read.

t_ch: 杂志每期都会评论匹诺康尼的当红电影，用语犀利、视角独特，从不向主流市场献媚。
t_en: Every issue has a review of the latest popular movies in Penacony, with sharp critiques and insightful perspectives that never pander to a mainstream audience.

t_ch: 你以为，如此优秀的期刊会请谁来当主笔？
t_en: Who do you think such an outstanding periodical would hire as its chief editor?

t_ch: 很有名吗？
t_en: Is it famous?

t_ch: 销量如何？
t_en: How are the sales?

t_ch: …又是这种态度、就是这种态度！你、你们…就因为大部分观众都是你这种水准……
t_en: ...There it is, this old attitude again! Y—You... because most of the audience are on the same level as you...

t_ch: 俗不可耐！艺术的事，能用销量这种东西来衡量吗？！
t_en: How vulgar! Art cannot be measured by something as trivial as sales!

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 哼，可算能安静会儿了……
t_en: Hmph, some peace and quiet at last...

t_ch: 我还有事要忙。
t_en: I have other matters to attend to.

t_ch: 那还不快去！和老头子我在这耗什么呢！
t_en: Then hurry off! What are you doing with a doddering old fool like me!

t_ch: 唉…世风日下，人心不古……
t_en: *sigh*... The world's not what it used to be. Kids these days...

t_ch: …什、什么…怎么回事…呃…呜啊啊啊——
t_en: ...Wh—What... What is going on? Er... wahhh...

t_ch: 眼前的男子突然开始嚎啕大哭起来——成年人的崩溃往往只在一瞬间。
t_en: The man suddenly starts to shriek out loud — the adult's entire collapse is in mere seconds.

t_ch: …不对（抽噎声）…不是这样的…我没有……
t_en: ...No *sobs*... Not like this... I didn't...

t_ch: （持续的哽咽与抽泣声）
t_en: (more choking and sobbing)

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: （抽噎声）…不…不！（抽噎声）……
t_en: *Chokes*... No... no! *Chokes*...

t_ch: …快去、去找家族…找梦境…鼓励师……
t_en: ...Go quickly, find The Family... Find the Dreamscape... Motivator...

t_ch: （持续的哽咽与抽泣声）
t_en: (more choking and sobbing)

t_ch: …找梦境…鼓励师……（持续的哽咽与抽泣声）
t_en: ...Find the Dreamscape... Motivator... *chokes* *sobs*

t_ch: <color=#f29e38ff>艾迪恩公园</color>那边好像有梦境鼓励师当班……
t_en: Pretty sure there's a Dreamscape Motivator on duty over by <color=#f29e38ff>Aideen Park</color>...

t_ch: …我们的友谊已经走到尽头啦！我受够了，这次一定要和他绝交！
t_en: ...Our friendship has run its course! I've had enough of this. It's time to break off relations with him!

t_ch: 我能明白您的感受，一直以来真是辛苦您……
t_en: I can understand how you feel. It's been so hard on you this whole time...

t_ch: 打扰一下，紧急情况！
t_en: Excuse me, emergency!

t_ch: 你将夏布罗的情况简单概括了一下，讲给安娜。
t_en: You briefly outline Chabro's situation for Anna.

t_ch: 如您所言，那位先生的状态确实有些糟糕…但我现在……
t_en: As you say, that gentleman's indeed in a bit of a pickle... But right now I...

t_ch: 哦…哦，我没关系，你先去吧。
t_en: Oh... Oh, that's fine. You go on.

t_ch: 感谢您的体谅，卡尔先生。那我先去确认一下情况，回来再联系您。
t_en: Thank you for your understanding, Mr. Carl. I'll double-check everything then and call you once I get back.

t_ch: 你知道吗…他曾经对我而言是最重要的人——是他毫不吝啬地将自己的研究成果与我分享，将我引向「智识」的正途。
t_en: You know what... He used to be the most important person in my life — he shared his research results with me without hesitation and led me along the correct Path of Erudition.

t_ch: 可就是这样一个人，怎么如今变得如此固执己见、如此故步自封了呢？
t_en: But how could someone like that somehow become so stubborn now?

t_ch: 你知道吗？他评判我的研究成果时那副态度，简直就像在教育一个不成器的孩子！
t_en: You know what? The attitude he showed when judging my research results was like lecturing an underperforming child!
Er...

t_ch: 抱歉，我只是忍不住牢骚几句…您快去吧，他们还在等您呢。
t_en: Sorry, I just can't help but complain... Go on. They're still waiting for you.

t_ch: …我透不过气来…全都是惨叫…太吵了！
t_en: ...I can't breathe... everything's screaming... it's too loud!

t_ch: 您先冷静一下，再这样下去很危险……
t_en: Please calm down. It's dangerous if you go on like this...

t_ch: …火…火…还在燃烧…从未熄灭……
t_en: ...Fire... the fire... It still burns... It never stopped burning...

t_ch: …烧吧、烧吧！！！
t_en: Burn... BURN!!!

t_ch: 随着夏布罗歇斯底里的惨叫，你们面前竟真出现了*一团火*。
t_en: As Chabro screams hysterically, a "ball of fire" actually appears in front of you.

t_ch: …不…不——别过来！
t_en: ...No... No! Stay away!

t_ch: 救命啊——！
t_en: Heeeelp!

t_ch: …咳…总、总之，这次给你添麻烦了。要是之后遇到什么困扰，可以来找我。
t_en: ...*Ahem*... A—Anyway, sorry for any trouble caused this time around. If you have any problems later on, you can come and find me.

t_ch: 老头子我好歹也算个有头有脸的人物，应该多少能帮上些忙。
t_en: I'm more or less a respectable enough old chap, and should be able to help you out with a few things still.

t_ch: …呼…吓死我了——
t_en: ...Phew... That scared me half to death...

t_ch: 这老先生可真不得了，竟然直接将*意识*投射成*实体*了……
t_en: That old guy is really something. I can't believe he has projected his "consciousness" into an "entity"...

t_ch: …抱、抱歉……
t_en: ...I—I'm sorry...

t_ch: 不过也好，现在负面情绪都被*物理*消灭了，老先生也就没有什么问题啦。
t_en: But that's fine. Now that the negative emotions have all been "physically" cleared away, the old chap won't have any more problems.

t_ch: 我先走了，卡尔先生还在等我呢。哦对了，刚刚谢谢你哦，有机会请你大餐！
t_en: I'll be off then. Mr. Carl is still waiting for me. Oh yes, thanks very much for that just then. I'll have to treat you to a good meal next time!

t_ch: 梦想是伟大的，艺术是永恒的……
t_en: Dreams are great, but art is eternal...

t_ch: 有时候我真庆幸自己来到了匹诺康尼…因为在梦里就可以不用再做梦了。
t_en: Sometimes I'm really glad that I came here to Penacony... Because I don't have to worry about having any dreams when I'm inside my dream.

t_ch: 你知道吗，孩子，在成为影评家之前，我也曾是一名导演…一名有梦想的导演。
t_en: You know what, kid? Before I became a critic, I was once a director... A director with his own dreams.

t_ch: 我也曾试图将自己对艺术的热爱、对生活的憧憬通过作品传达给人们、传达给整个世界，可……
t_en: I once tried, too, to pass on my love for art and lust for life to the people — to the whole world — through my films, but...

t_ch: 如今我已许久不曾做梦了，它们在我停止创作的那一天便离我而去了。
t_en: These days I don't really dream anymore. They more or less left me the day that I stopped creating.

t_ch: 至于那些我曾因自己的激情与梦想所取得的「荣耀」…那令我不得不远走他乡、隐姓埋名的「荣耀」…曾有太多人为它奉献、为它牺牲——
t_en: And as for the "glory" that I've achieved with all my passion and dreams... That same "glory" forced me to travel far away, keeping anonymous the whole time... too many people have devoted themselves for that — sacrificed themselves for that...

t_ch: 即便我至今仍无法直面它们，却始终都背负着它们，直到生命的尽头……
t_en: Even though I'm unable to face them, I will always carry them with me for the rest of my days...

t_ch: 夏布罗此刻看起来分外平和，就连脸上的褶子都仿佛不那么锋利了。
t_en: Chabro looks extremely at peace right then. Even the wrinkles cracked across his face seemed to be less sharp.

t_ch: 咳，之前让你见笑了…我是说那个广告牌的事——我那会儿正在气头上，你别介意。
t_en: *Ahem* Well, I made you laugh before... I mean, about the billboard... But I was just furious at the time. Please don't take it to heart.

t_ch: 但实话实话，匹诺康尼的影视圈现在简直一团乌烟瘴气！看看现在的那些后辈，简直一蟹不如一蟹！
t_en: But honestly, Penacony's movie industry is simply in tatters at the moment! Just look at the younger generation now. They're getting ever worse by the year!

t_ch: 一个个本事没多少，脾气大得不得了；鼻子翘上天，不懂得尊师重道。那些人能拍出什么好片子才怪！
t_en: None of them have any talent, and they're all extremely short-tempered. Yet they look down on everyone and don't know how to respect their teachers. What kind of film is someone like that going to shoot, I ask you!

t_ch: 你看着倒是个好苗子…怎么样，有兴趣进军影视圈吗？老头子我保证能把你培养成知名导演。
t_en: You seem like a good prospect... How about it, are you interested in joining the ranks of the cinema circle? I guarantee to train you up to become a famous director.

t_ch: 先别急着拒绝，来，看看这个——新鲜出炉的《银幕冲击波》！来好好见识一下老头子我的能耐吧！
t_en: Don't be so quick to shoot me down. Take a look at this freshly released Silver Screen Shockwave! Come see what this old man can do!

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的影视圈现在简直一团乌烟瘴气！看看现在的那些后辈，简直一蟹不如一蟹！
t_en: Penacony's movie industry is simply in tatters at the moment! Just look at the younger generation now. They're getting ever worse by the year!

t_ch: 一个个本事没多少，脾气大得不得了；鼻子翘上天，不懂得尊师重道。那些人能拍出什么好片子才怪！
t_en: None of them have any talent, and they're all extremely short-tempered. Yet they look down on everyone and don't know how to respect their teachers. What kind of film is someone like that going to shoot, I ask you!

t_ch: 你看着倒是个好苗子…怎么样，有兴趣进军影视圈吗？老头子我保证能把你培养成知名导演。
t_en: You seem like a good prospect... How about it, are you interested in joining the ranks of the cinema circle? I guarantee to train you up to become a famous director.

t_ch: 人这一生啊，可真是兜兜转转……
t_en: *sigh* Life... We just go round and round in circles...

t_ch: 哈哈，你知道吗，我虽然是个导演，这一生最辉煌的时刻却并不在颁奖台上。
t_en: Haha, you know what? I may be a director, but the most glorious moment in all my life was never up there on that awards stage.

t_ch: 我曾与星夜大奖擦肩而过——彼时我还以为那是会是我人生中最难忘的记忆。
t_en: I once rubbed shoulders with the stars at the Starcademy Awards... Back then, I thought it would be the most unforgettable memory I ever had.

t_ch: 但后来我却反倒因此幡然醒悟，明白了镜头能够传达的东西总归有限，有些东西需要亲自让人们体验才行。
t_en: But later on, I suddenly woke up one day and realized that the camera lens is always going to be limited in what it can depict, and some things have to be experienced by people for themselves.

t_ch: …我曾听说某些星球上，人们会在秋冬之际烧毁荒芜的杂草，只为来年能在这片土地上培养出优质的粮食。
t_en: ...I once heard of some planets where the people would burn up their dried weeds during the winter months, just to nourish high-quality crops on the land in the year ahead.

t_ch: 于是三年后，我又来到了奖台上——带着智慧的火种，用灵魂的热度点燃了会场、点燃了人群！
t_en: And so three years later, I arrived at the award podium once more — with the fire of wisdom and using the heat of my soul, I ignited the whole arena and set off the whole audience!

t_ch: 我始终坚信在灰烬之中，将会诞生更高尚的萌芽…而果然无论是那一届星夜奖，还是我自己，都因此红透了半边天……
t_en: I'm a firm believer that out of the ashes can sprout an even more exalted future... and sure enough, both the Starcademy Awards and I spread our fame like wildfire...

t_ch: 你知道吗，孩子——时至今日，星际间依旧传扬着我的大名！
t_en: You know what, kid? To this day, my name is still passed around among the stars!

t_ch: 一群乌合之众……
t_en: A real motley crew...

t_ch: 陈腐、愚钝、俗不可耐！我不明白，为什么市场会变成这样。
t_en: So clichéd, stupid, and vulgar! I don't get it. How did the market ever get like this?

t_ch: 制片人？嗬，额头下长的眼是钱眼，脑子里灌的浆是泥浆！
t_en: A producer? Wow — someone who's got cash for eyes, and sludge for brains!

t_ch: 观众？哈，与其说他们有审美，不如说有种神经坏死的美。
t_en: The audience? Ha, it's not so much that they have aesthetic taste, insofar as it's a kind of nervous necrotic beauty.

t_ch: 镜头单调故事平庸毫无个性就为能让观众看懂，这种人拍什么电影！
t_en: The shots are mundane. The story is average, with no personality for ease of understanding. What kind of movie is this person trying to shoot!?

t_ch: 一个隐喻一个伏笔还得创作者下场解释八百遍，这种人看什么电影！
t_en: Every metaphor or foreshadowing has to be explained eight hundred times by the creator. What kind of movies does this type of person watch!?

t_ch: 说什么要我去迎合什么主流市场、什么大众审美…就是因为…就因为全都是这种水准，我才……
t_en: Asking me to pander to the mainstream market, and pop culture... It's exactly because... because they're all of this kind of standard that I...

t_ch: 哼，如今就连这所谓的「梦想之城」也不过如此。何其可悲啊——在这无药可救的庸俗之中，艺术又该如何立足！
t_en: Hmph, even this so-called City of Dreams is like that nowadays. How sad — how could art ever hope to stand up among the hopeless vulgarity of this world!

t_ch: 哎——不用一天到晚挂着个假笑、对宾客点头哈腰的感觉可真好！
t_en: Hey — it feels great not having to wear this fake smile and grovel for guests all day!

t_ch: 就是看不到领导们的滑稽嘴脸、不能怼人稍微有点无聊……
t_en: It's just, things are kind of boring if I can't see the funny faces of the bosses, and I can't diss people...

t_ch: 确实挺好。
t_en: It's really good actually.

t_ch: 细说怼人。
t_en: Tell me more about this dissing.

t_ch: 哎哟你谁啊！突然冒出来吓我一跳…多大的人了，家里没教过你不要在街上乱搭话吗？
t_en: Ack, who are you!? Suddenly popping up out of nowhere... You nearly gave me a heart attack! How old are you? Didn't your parents ever teach you not to talk to strangers on the streets?

t_ch: 可我还是个孩子。
t_en: But I'm still a child.

t_ch: 那你这孩子…个头可真不小。算了，看在我今儿心情好的份上，和你聊聊也不是不行。
t_en: Then you're... pretty big for a kid. Whatever, seeing as I'm in such a good mood today, I can chat a little while with you.

t_ch: 哎，我和你说啊，以后要是找工作，可千万别干酒店前台。
t_en: Hey, I tell you what — if you're ever looking for a job, never work as a hotel receptionist.

t_ch: 为什么？听我给你讲讲做前台的「好处」就知道了——
t_en: Why? Just let me tell you about the "benefits" of working as a hotel receptionist...

t_ch: 闲暇太少、规矩太多；客人太吵闹，领导太啰嗦。
t_en: No leisure, too many rules, noisy guests, and bossy bosses.

t_ch: 还有家族的同事，上班正事不干，下班喝酒团建；不参与等于不合群，他们所谓的「同谐」也就这点水平了。
t_en: Plus some staff members are from The Family and do zero work, then drink all night after work. If you don't go, they boot you out of their group, which doesn't seem to uphold "Harmony," if you ask me.

t_ch: 那还不转行？
t_en: Why don't you switch careers?

t_ch: 嗨，我倒是想——但多亏了我那「亲爱的」姐姐，自己在现实酒店当前台就算了，还非得买一赠一，把我也安排上。
t_en: Ha, I'd love to — but thanks to my "darling" sister, I've been signed up to work at the hotel in reality as a receptionist alongside her. It's not enough for her to be working there. She had to make it a two-for-one deal.

t_ch: 嘿…你知道吗，那家伙其实做梦都想在梦境酒店工作。不过——可惜啊，她连梦都做不了。
t_en: Ha... Do you know what? She actually dreams of working at the hotel in the dreamscape. But how pathetic — she can't even dream properly.

t_ch: 我就喜欢看她明明嫉妒我、却又不得不装出一幅关心我的嘴脸，嘻嘻…真是我的好姐姐。
t_en: I just like the look of her being so obviously jealous of me yet still having to pretend to care, teehee... That's my big sis.

t_ch: 来聊聊吗？
t_en: Care to chat?

t_ch: 行啊，反正我这会儿也闲着没事。你想聊什么？
t_en: Sure, in any case, there's nothing going on right now. What do you want to chat about?

t_ch: 所以你这是在休假？
t_en: So, are you here on vacation?

t_ch: 对啊，毕竟梦境酒店已经被封锁了嘛…你应该拿到梦境护照了吧？宾客须知上应该有写才对。
t_en: Yeah, after all, the hotel in the Dreamscape is under lockdown now... You've got a Dreamscape Pass, right? It should be in the Guest Notice.

t_ch: 这次封锁发生得还真突然，而且还是一刀切，没有任何回旋余地。据说是家主直接下的命令，不论家族的人还是逐梦客都一股脑被赶了出来，一概不准进入酒店了。
t_en: The lockdown happened so fast and caught everyone up in the same net, with no wiggle room for anybody. I heard it was a direct order coming down from the family head. Both The Family and all Dreamchasers — everyone was booted out with nobody allowed back into the hotel.

t_ch: 我还挺好奇那里面究竟发生了什么的。不过好奇归好奇，我可不想被卷进奇怪的麻烦里…所以还是不瞎打听了吧。
t_en: I'm pretty curious about what happened in there. That's one thing, but I don't want to get caught up in any weird trouble... so I'd better stop asking around so much I guess.

t_ch: 工资照发吗？
t_en: Are you on paid leave?

t_ch: 那当然——别的不说，家族在这方面还是很贴心的。
t_en: Of course — If anything, The Family treats us pretty well when it comes to that.

t_ch: 这种天上掉下来的假期，可得好好享受才不算浪费啊。
t_en: A vacation out of nowhere like this should not be wasted and should be fully taken advantage of.

t_ch: 你的职能很重要吗？
t_en: Is your role very important?

t_ch: 这话问的…看不起谁呢！
t_en: You even hear yourself? Don't you look down on me!

t_ch: 不是自夸，我可是这一届的风云人物。你知道匹诺康尼本地有多少人觊觎我这个岗位吗？不夸张地说，每次扩招都争得头破血流！
t_en: Not to brag, but I'm the woman of the moment right now. Do you know how many people there are in Penacony who eye this job of mine? I'm not kidding when I say there's a raucous riot every time this position is hiring!

t_ch: 悄悄告诉你，鸢尾花家的家主特别器重我——我也知道自己脾气冲、说话直，但在那位大人眼里，这些可都是优点！
t_en: And let me tell you on the down low, the head of the Iris Family holds me in high regard. I know I can be brash and blunt, but to the head, these are all pluses!

t_ch: 你们姐妹的关系……
t_en: About your relationship with your sister...

t_ch: 我们的关系，最好就是没关系。
t_en: The best relationship we could have is no relationship.

t_ch: 别看她平时装得多正经，内心其实不知道有多阴暗。
t_en: Never you mind how proper and serious she goes about looking. You have no idea how dark her innermost thoughts can get.

t_ch: 她这种人啊，永远都不会知道「美梦」究竟是什么滋味…活该！
t_en: People like her will never know the feeling of "sweet dreams"... and it's all her fault!

t_ch: 就先聊到这吧。
t_en: Let's end it here for now.

t_ch: 啊？我还没聊够呢……
t_en: Oh? But I'm still not done...

t_ch: 离开
t_en: Leave

t_ch: 这就要走了？行吧，有空再聊。和你闲聊还挺有意思的。
t_en: Going so soon? Fine, let's chat again next time you're free. You're fun to talk to.

t_ch: 哎——不用一天到晚挂着个假笑、对宾客点头哈腰的感觉可真好！
t_en: Hey — it feels great not having to wear this fake smile and grovel for guests all day!

t_ch: 你知道吗，我这个姐姐啊，从小就视我为眼中钉、肉中刺。
t_en: You know what? Since we were young, my older sister always saw me as this thorn in her side.

t_ch: 年纪不大的时候就抢我的零食、弄坏我的玩具，还骗走了我所有的朋友……
t_en: When we were still small, she'd steal snacks off me, break my toys, and steal away all of my friends...

t_ch: 等年纪大了呢，因为亏心事做得太多，被自己折磨得夜不能寐，就干脆变卖了所有积蓄，跑到这个所谓的「梦想之城」来。
t_en: Once we got a little bit older, the evil deeds racked up day after day to the point where she couldn't sleep at night. She decided to sell off everything she owned, and come to this so-called City of Dreams.

t_ch: 可即便如此又能怎样呢？到头来她还是「不敢做梦」，啧啧，真是自作自受啊。
t_en: Yeah — but so what? In the end, she still "dared not dream." Tsk tsk, she really can only blame herself.

t_ch: 只可怜我被她牵连，不得不留在这个吵闹的梦里。哎，做梦有什么好呢？我一点都不喜欢什么美梦，我只想踏踏实实地睡上一觉。
t_en: It's just a pity that I got roped in by her and had to stay here in this noisy dream. *sigh* What good are dreams? I don't care in the slightest for sweet dreams. I just want a solid night's sleep.

t_ch: 自私自利的恶毒女人…我宁愿跟一块顽石当姐妹，也不想和她多相处一秒。
t_en: What a vicious, calculating woman... I'd rather be sister to a stubborn donkey than spend another second with her.

t_ch: 这话说得有点绝。
t_en: That's a little bit absolute.

t_ch: 哦，你没法理解，我不怪你。这种感受…有些事不曾亲身经历过的话，确实很难理解。
t_en: Oh, you have no way of understanding, and I don't blame you. This feeling... Some things are impossible to get unless you experience them for yourself.

t_ch: 如果真的想知道我的感受，那就回现实和她聊聊吧。这样你就知道她究竟有多虚伪了。
t_en: If you really want to know how I feel, then go back out to reality and talk to her. That way you'll know for yourself what a hypocrite she is.

t_ch: 她在现实中的「白日梦」酒店当<color=#f29e38ff>前台接待</color>。面对那种无聊的工作，我猜她每天肯定都过得愁眉苦脸的吧？嘻嘻……
t_en: She works <color=#f29e38ff>as a receptionist</color> out in The Reverie in reality. Such boring work should leave her feeling utterly miserable each day, right? Tee-hee...

t_ch: 如果真的想知道我的感受，那就回现实和她聊聊吧。这样你就知道她究竟有多虚伪了。
t_en: If you really want to know how I feel, then go back out to reality and talk to her. That way you'll know for yourself what a hypocrite she is.

t_ch: 她在现实中的「白日梦」酒店当<color=#f29e38ff>前台接待</color>。面对那种无聊的工作，我猜她每天肯定都过得愁眉苦脸的吧？嘻嘻……
t_en: She works <color=#f29e38ff>as a receptionist</color> out in The Reverie in reality. Such boring work should leave her feeling utterly miserable each day, right? Tee-hee...

t_ch: 您好，请问有什么需要吗？
t_en: Greetings. Is there something you need?

t_ch: 关于你妹妹……
t_en: I'm here about your little sister...

t_ch: 哎呀，那孩子给您添了什么麻烦吗？
t_en: *sigh* Did that kid cause any trouble for you?

t_ch: 实在是抱歉，我妹妹性格比较直率，心智也不够成熟。如果她不小心冒犯了您，请允许我这个做姐姐的代她向您赔礼……
t_en: I'm really sorry. My sister can be blunt and isn't always the most mature. If she has accidentally offended you, then please allow me — as her sister — to make it up to on her behalf...

t_ch: 她没得罪我。
t_en: She hasn't offended me.

t_ch: 真的吗？太好了…唉，您不知道，奎茵那孩子，之前就没少因为口无遮拦得罪别人。
t_en: Really? That's great... *sigh* You have no idea how many people Quinn has offended because she doesn't know how to keep her mouth shut.

t_ch: 她呀，多大的人了，还一直表现得像个小孩子似的…整天让我为她操心。
t_en: At her age, she's still behaving as though she's still a little kid... Always making me worry about her all day long.

t_ch: 听说你们关系不好？
t_en: I heard your relationship isn't the best?

t_ch: 是奎茵和您说的吧？也不奇怪，她从小就是这个性格…还时不时就喜欢给我找点麻烦。
t_en: Is that what Quinn said? I'm not surprised, as she's had this kind of personality since she was small... She likes causing trouble for me every now and again.

t_ch: 但即便如此，我们也是爱着彼此的…我们姐妹之间的联系牢不可破。她<color=#f29e38ff>永远都是我最重要的妹妹</color>。
t_en: But even so, we also love each other... Our sisterly bond is unbreakable. She will <color=#f29e38ff>always be my sister and the most important person to me</color>.

t_ch: 听上去，您似乎和奎茵相处得不错。那么，您可以听听我的不情之请吗？
t_en: Sounds like you and Quinn are getting along like a house on fire. So, if I may be so bold as to ask you a favor?

t_ch: 那孩子因为性格的原因没什么朋友，我工作又太忙，很难和她见上一面。
t_en: That kid has no real friends due to her personality issues, and I'm too busy with my work, so I never get a chance to see her.

t_ch: 要是不嫌弃的话，您若什么时候得了空闲，可以替我陪陪她吗？
t_en: If you don't mind and if you have any free time, would you please keep her company for me?

t_ch: 眼前的奎茵看起来分外平静，你却莫名能够感受到她心中的愤怒。
t_en: Quinn may look extremely calm, but you can feel a kind of rage deep in her heart.

t_ch: 就像是煤灰堆下隐约透出的暗火。
t_en: Just like a smolder of flame burning dimly beneath a pile of soot.

t_ch: 让我猜猜她是怎么和你说的——
t_en: Let me guess what she told you...

t_ch: 「那孩子给你添麻烦了吧？她还小不懂事，我替她道歉」——虚伪！
t_en: "Did that kid cause any trouble for you? She's young and still doesn't know squat. Please let me apologize on behalf of her"... Hypocrite!

t_ch: 「我们姐妹的关系其实很好，我们超~爱~彼此的」——做作！
t_en: "Our sisterly relationship is actually really great, and we LOVE each other SO much~" ...Liar!

t_ch: 「哎呀我太忙了没时间，有空你替我陪陪她吧」——令人作呕！
t_en: "Shucks. I'm so busy and don't have any time. Could you please keep her company" ...Gross!

t_ch: 她还说了其它什么吗？没有了吧…每次都是这一套，呵，也不嫌腻歪。
t_en: Did she say anything else? Nothing, right... It's always the same old trick each time, ha. Doesn't she ever get bored?

t_ch: 是不是有什么误会……
t_en: Has there been some kind of misunderstanding here...

t_ch: 等等，你该不会…真的信了她的鬼话吧？饶了我吧还是。
t_en: Hang on, you don't actually... believe her lies? Spare me, please!

t_ch: 我恨她，她也恨我——这是无可改变的事实。
t_en: I hate her, and she hates me... That's the incontrovertible truth.

t_ch: 我*这辈子*都在想方设法摆脱她造就的阴影，而她则是这个世界上…最希望我这个负担彻底消失的人。
t_en: I've spent my *entire life* trying to get out from under her shadow. She's the one person in this world... who hopes that this burden — me — one day disappears forever.

t_ch: …要不你们好好聊聊？
t_en: ...Why don't you guys sit down for a nice chat?

t_ch: 聊聊？还真是轻描淡写…不，我们永远都没法好好聊，以前不可能，现在更不可能！
t_en: A nice chat!? You're really downplaying things... No, we can never have a nice chat — it was impossible before, and it's even more so now!

t_ch: 你觉得我是在耍小脾气，是吧？你觉得我这是任性、蛮横、无理取闹，是吧？
t_en: You just think I'm sulking here, don't you? You think I'm headstrong, rude, and causing a scene — right?

t_ch: 但我告诉你，我告诉你——我说我们*永远*也没法<color=#f29e38ff>面对面</color>聊，而她就是那个原因！
t_en: But I'm telling you — we will *never* be able to chat <color=#f29e38ff>face-to-face</color>, and it's all her fault!

t_ch: 你以为我不想回现实看看吗？不巧，我早就是个幽灵了——我根本离不开这该死的梦境，我的人生已经结束了，没戏唱了——你懂吗？
t_en: Do you think I don't want to go back to reality? Unfortunately I've already been a spirit for a long time — I can't leave this cursed dreamscape. My life is over, totally over — do you understand what I'm saying here?

t_ch: 你以为她敢到梦境里来找我吗？不…艾丽，那家伙，她根本没有胆量直视我的眼睛。直到今天，她也还是在逃避！
t_en: Do you think she dares come into the dreamscape to pay me a visit? No... Alley doesn't have the guts to look me straight in the eye. To this day, she's still been avoiding it!

t_ch: 我这双眼啊…就是在她眼前彻底失去了光亮。我敢发誓，她这辈子都忘不了——
t_en: These eyes of mine... they've completely lost their brightness due to her. I swear, she is going to never forget...

t_ch: ——忘不了当年她是怎么把自己的亲生妹妹推下楼梯的。嘻嘻……
t_en: ...never forget how she pushed her own sister down the stairs. Heehee...

t_ch: 奎茵看起来分外平静，你却莫名能够感受到她心中的怒气——就像是煤灰堆下隐约透出的暗火。
t_en: Quinn may look extremely calm, but you can feel a kind of rage deep in her heart — just like a smolder of flame burning dimly beneath a pile of soot.

t_ch: 怎么，还有什么想知道的？这个够了吗？
t_en: Why, is there anything else you'd like to know? Is that enough?

t_ch: 你知道吗？我时不时就能回忆起当时的情景。
t_en: You know what? Sometimes I think about what happened back then.

t_ch: …很疼…非常非常疼…「我」的心跳很得快，却喘不上气来……
t_en: ...It hurt... so much... "My" heart was beating so fast, yet I couldn't breathe...

t_ch: 那时候我们才刚刚大吵了一架，她眼里的怒火还没烧干净，就被暴风雨似的惊恐浇灭了，湿淋淋的。
t_en: We'd just had this big fight, and before the anger in her eyes had burnt out, it was doused in a storm-like panic, utterly drenched.

t_ch: 我一动也动不了，连喊疼的力气都没有，只能紧紧盯着那双眼睛。视线随之变得模糊…不知不觉间，「我」就睡着了。
t_en: I couldn't move. I didn't even have the strength to cry out in pain — I just stared at those eyes. My vision grew blurry... Before I knew it, "I" was asleep.

t_ch: 再次睁开眼睛，「我」已经身处黄金的时刻，当起梦境中的酒店前台啦。
t_en: The next time I opened my eyes, "I" was already in the golden hour as a receptionist at the hotel in the dreamscape.

t_ch: 故事是不是还挺精彩的？可惜啊，都是*假的*——我的记忆、我的痛苦、甚至我的存在本身，全都是那个女人自欺欺人的谎言！
t_en: Isn't this story really something? It's a pity everything is *fake* — my memory, my pain, and even my remaining existence. It's all lies that she tells us and herself!

t_ch: 多可笑，多可悲啊…你说是吧？
t_en: How ridiculous! How tragic... Don't you think?

t_ch: 此刻的奎茵面无表情，一双眼睛仿佛初冬清晨泛着苍白雾气的湖面。
t_en: Right then, Quinn's face wore no emotion. Here eyes were like cool morning mist on a wintry lake.

t_ch: 我受够了…真的受够了……
t_en: I've had enough... Really, I've had enough...

t_ch: 为什么早已死去的人不能安息，还活着的又非得扮作一个虚假的影子，佯装对方还在梦中苟延残喘呢？
t_en: Why can't the dead rest in peace? And why do the living have to pretend to be this fake shadow, pretending that we're struggling on in this dreamscape here?

t_ch: 愧疚有那么重要吗？悔恨有那么持久吗？
t_en: Is guilt really that important? Is remorse really that permanent?

t_ch: 一切都为了她荒唐可笑的自我满足！都为了她毫无意义的自我折磨！
t_en: And all for her ridiculous ego! All because of her meaningless self-torture!

t_ch: 我太累了…真的…太累了，她什么时候才能放过自己、放过「我」呢……
t_en: I'm tired... Really, I'm tired. When is she going to let go of herself? When is she going to let go of "me"...

t_ch: 奎茵的神色有一瞬恍惚，但随即又变回最初那种生动活泼。
t_en: Quinn's face looks stunned for a second, but soon returns to as lively as it was before.

t_ch: 嗯…我们刚刚说到哪来着…哎呦，怎么这么累啊？
t_en: Well... What were we just talking about... *sigh* Why am I so tired?

t_ch: 肯定是平时工作被压榨得太狠，才和你聊了多大会儿啊，就好像一晚上没睡觉似的。
t_en: It must be because I'm getting ruthlessly put through the wringer at work, and that's why I've blabbed on so much. It's kinda like when you stay up all night.

t_ch: 好在我刚买的钟表披萨还没吃完，赶紧补充一下碳水…哈，我猜也是白费力气，梦境里哪有什么营养学的说法
t_en: Luckily I've still got some of this Clockie Pizza left, so I can stock up on carbs... Ha, I guess it's all waste of time actually, as there's no such thing as nutrition inside the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 嗯？看我干嘛，你也想吃？好吧，看在我们聊得还算开心的份上…分你一块也不是不行。
t_en: Well, what are you looking at? Hungry, too? Alright, seeing as we've been chatting nicely... it's not like I can't share some of this with you.

t_ch: 嗯…我们刚刚说到哪来着…哎呦，怎么这么累啊？
t_en: Well... What were we just talking about... *sigh* Why am I so tired?

t_ch: 肯定是平时工作被压榨得太狠，才和你聊了多大会儿啊，就好像一晚上没睡觉似的。
t_en: It must be because I'm getting ruthlessly put through the wringer at work, and that's why I've blabbed on so much. It's kinda like when you stay up all night.

t_ch: 难得的假期，就该好好放松一下——不然连我自己的良心都会谴责我的。
t_en: It's your own hard-earned vacation, so relax a little... Otherwise, even my own conscience is going to condemn me.

t_ch: 奎茵目视虚空，神色宁静，略带笑容，仿佛陷入某种难以言喻的喜悦之中。
t_en: Quinn stares off into space in serenity with a slight smile, as if experiencing some kind of indescribable joy.

t_ch: 这喜悦似冬至草木凋敝，似日落天光为影，似久经漂泊的疲惫灵魂将在终途驻停。
t_en: This joy is like the withered crops of winter solstice; like the sky turning to shade as the sun sets; like the tired soul coming to a stop after a lifetime of wandering.

t_ch: 真好，终于可以休息了……
t_en: It's wonderful. I can finally rest...

t_ch: 真不容易，难得有那么一刻，我觉得自己的内心没有那么沉重了。
t_en: It's not easy to meet a moment like this where my heart doesn't feel as though it's so heavy.

t_ch: 让我休息吧…我太累了。如果可以，我真的不想再醒来了……
t_en: Let me rest a while... I'm tired. And if I might, I really don't feel like ever waking up again...

t_ch: 哟，你好啊。我是科琳，筑梦师中的点子王，脑袋里的梦境素材能堆满整个匹诺康尼——还不重样！
t_en: Yo, how is it going? It's me, Colleen — the idea hamster for the Dreamweavers. I've got enough Dreamscape material in my head to fill all of Penacony — you want it, I got it!

t_ch: 我的本职是个宇宙行商，经常开着飞船到处鬼混。你也可以叫我「科琳船长」。
t_en: I'm a traveling merchant by trade, and fly my ship anywhere I please. You can call me "Captain Colleen."

t_ch: …船长？
t_en: ..."Captain"?

t_ch: 哈，看我的装扮不像，是吧？
t_en: Ha, can't tell judging by my outfit, can you?

t_ch: 我承认，这一身确实贵气了些。不过来这种地方要想不被人看扁，多少还是得注意些扮相的…要是我把平时那身行头穿来，估计要吓坏不少人。
t_en: I admit, this look is indeed a little more bling. But if you don't want to be looked down upon in a place like this, you should pay attention to dressing up... It would freak out the lot of you if I were to wear my usual get-up here.

t_ch: 你先接受这个设定就行——要是咱俩处得来，你自然能见识到我「专业」的一面。
t_en: Just accept this for now — and if we get along well, you'll naturally catch my "professional" side.

t_ch: 梦境素材？
t_en: Dreamscape material?

t_ch: 梦境嘛，本质上是人们现实经历的糅合重组。一辈子不出门的家伙，能梦见的也无非自己那一亩三分地上发生的无聊事。
t_en: The Dreamscape: Basically a remix and reshuffle of people's real life experiences. Someone who never goes outside to touch grass can only dream of whatever boredom that happens around their own little patch of turf.

t_ch: 想让梦境有趣但不至于扯淡，家族就必须找一位走过南闯过北的顾问才行。
t_en: To make the Dreamscape interesting without being nonsense, The Family had to find a consultant — someone who was very well-traveled.

t_ch: 原本最适合做这活的是无名客，谁料到阿基维利那倒霉蛋死得早，那帮人紧跟着销声匿迹了…少了这个第一选择，宇宙行商无疑是最靠谱的次选——所以他们邀请了我。
t_en: At first, the best candidate for the job was the Nameless. But unexpectedly, that poor chap Akivili ended up kicking the bucket, and the rest of the gang disappeared without a trace soon after... Without this first-choice option available, a traveling merchant was the most reliable — and so they invited me.

t_ch: 说到底，这宇宙中还有多少人配得上「无名客」的称呼呢？
t_en: After all, how many people in this universe are worthy of the "Nameless" title?

t_ch: 我们星穹列车应该可以？
t_en: What about us aboard the Astral Express?

t_ch: 等会儿，你是…星穹列车的成员？它现在情况如何了？
t_en: Hang on, are you... a member of the Astral Express? How are things there right now?

t_ch: 你向科琳概述了星穹列车一路的经历。
t_en: You share with Colleen an overview of your experiences aboard the Astral Express.

t_ch: …真令人感慨，没想到这么多琥珀纪过去了，居然还有人行走在正统的「开拓」命途上。
t_en: ...That's really touching. I didn't expect that after so many Amber Eras there would still be people walking the Path of Trailblaze.

t_ch: …咳，失礼了。既然你已经为我讲了这么多经历，作为回报，我也该给你讲讲我的。
t_en: ...*ahem* I'm sorry. Since you've told me so much about your experiences, I should offer up the same as thanks and tell you a little about myself.

t_ch: 那么，你喜欢听什么样的故事？
t_en: So then, what kind of stories do you like best?

t_ch: 哟，你又来了，这次有什么打算吗？
t_en: Yo, you're back. What's the plan this time?

t_ch: 我想要听听你的经历。
t_en: I'd like to hear about your experiences.

t_ch: 好呀，科琳船长的故事多到说不完。你喜欢听什么样的故事？
t_en: Alright, Captain Colleen's got many a story. What kind of stories do you like best?

t_ch: 刺激一点的。
t_en: Thrilling ones.

t_ch: 刺激一点的…我想想……
t_en: Thrilling, eh... Let me see...

t_ch: 有一次，我受博识学会委托，前去回收位于「新伊甸」大黑洞附近的一个侦测信标。报酬非常优厚，所以我没多想就同意了。
t_en: There was this one time on an assignment from the Intelligentsia Guild, when we were retrieving a probe beacon from nearby this mega black hole called the New Eden. The pay was so fat that we agreed without thinking too much about it.

t_ch: 一去才发现，信标早就不在原来的位置了。某个意外让它脱离了固定点，正朝着黑洞所在的方向一路飘去，很快就会越过事件视界。
t_en: Upon arrival, we discovered that the beacon wasn't in its original spot. An accident had dragged it away from its fixed location, and it was drifting toward the black hole — soon to cross over the event horizon.

t_ch: 越过事件视界就相当于扎进巨真蛰虫的胃袋——有去无回。所以我必须在信标彻底跌入黑洞前把它截住…这真是我船长生涯中最棘手的挑战。
t_en: Crossing the event horizon is like plunging into the stomach of a Giant Sting: There's no coming back from it. So I had to intercept the beacon before it fell completely into the black hole... This was the most difficult challenge I ever encountered in my career as a captain.

t_ch: 要知道，事件视界之外也是有引力的，而且越靠近中心、引力就越强。我那艘船的发动机不是尖货，没法保证取到信标后还能出来……
t_en: Y'know what, there is gravity outside the event horizon, and it gets stronger the closer you get to the center. My ship's engine wasn't the best, and there was no guarantee that we could even get out once we'd nabbed the beacon...

t_ch: …好在信标的前进方向上有不少金属碎片。我可以从船上发射一个东西，与那些碎片碰撞、反弹，从而给信标施加一个往回来的力……
t_en: ...Luckily there were lots of metal scraps in the beacon's path. I could launch something from the ship to collide with the scraps and ricochet, thereby exerting a returning force onto the beacon...

t_ch: 这原理解释起来可就复杂了…知道{RUBY_B#台球}撞洞球{RUBY_E#}吗？就跟那个差不多，但我要打的是有史以来最精妙，最传奇的一杆！
t_en: This principle is quite hard to explain... Do you know {RUBY_B#Billiards}billiards{RUBY_E#}? It's just like that, but the shot I was gonna make would be the most exquisite, legendary shot in all history!

t_ch: 是啊，入射角度必须精确，发射力度必须适中；任何失误都会破坏碎片的排列，根本没有重来的余地。
t_en: Yeah, the angle of incidence must be exact with moderate force only; any mistakes would destroy the arrangement of the scraps. There was no room for a mulligan here.

t_ch: 不，要真那样的话就算不上惊险了……
t_en: No, if it were that alone, it wouldn't count as being thrilling...

t_ch: 是不是很好奇，为什么这样的故事称得上惊险呢？别急，我来告诉你……
t_en: Aren't you curious as to why this story isn't considered thrilling, though? Don't worry. I'll fill you in...

t_ch: 那一杆打得并不好。飞船发出的物体没有按照预想回弹，而是像没头苍蝇一样乱撞一气，反过来加速了信标的沉没。
t_en: The shot was no good. The missiles did not rebound as expected, but instead smashed around like a headless fly — which then accelerated the sinking of the beacon.

t_ch: 其实这个时候，我还是可以开足马力追上信标的。但那风险实在太大，不值得。
t_en: At this point, I could actually still speed in to catch up with the beacon. But the risk was too great to be worth it.

t_ch: 所以我调转船头，打算直接驶离这片星系…结果迎面撞上了一支泯灭帮的舰队。那群疯狗，追的那叫一个紧！我都怀疑他们是学会派来追杀我的。
t_en: And so I turned around the spaceship and planned to jet straight out of that star system... but ended up running straight into an Annihilation Gang fleet. Those crazy mutts were doing all they could to chase me down! I even suspected that perhaps the guild had sent them to hunt me down.

t_ch: 我的飞船在围攻中被华丽地炸成了碎片，我本人则借着爆风登上了泯灭帮的战舰。
t_en: My ship was blown into magnificent smithereens during the assault, and I used the force of the explosion to board the Annihilation Gang's battleship.

t_ch: 战斗过程暂且省略。最终，我成功制服了全部贼人，开着抢来的战舰一脚油门驶离了星系——这是最刺激的部分。
t_en: I won't go into details for now about the battle. In the end I managed to defeat all the bad guys, then put the pedal to the metal to pilot this stolen warship straight out of the star system — now THAT was the most thrilling part of the story.

t_ch: 听起来有点扯淡。
t_en: Sounds like nonsense.

t_ch: 呃…这里面涉及到很多暴力细节。我看你年纪轻轻，还是少听点不健康的内容为好。
t_en: Well... there was lots of violence in the details. I can tell you're young, though, so don't want to expose you to all that.

t_ch: …总之，动用你的想象力吧。你不是个开拓者吗？那就不该被现实束缚了思维！
t_en: ...Anyways, use your imagination! Aren't you a Trailblazer? If so, you shouldn't restrain your thinking to just reality!

t_ch: 太酷了！
t_en: Awesome!

t_ch: 是吧？大名鼎鼎的巡海游侠也做不到更好了。而这只是我传奇生涯中的一个小小注脚。
t_en: Right? Not even the illustrious Galaxy Rangers could do any better. And this is but one small, micro footnote in the annals of my legendary career.

t_ch: 有兴趣的话，之后再给你讲点其他的动作戏…等我花点时间编下细节。
t_en: If you're interested, I can spin a few more action stories for you later... just let me come up with some of the details first.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Keep silent)

t_ch: 呃，你就没有点…感想什么的？
t_en: Er, don't you have any... thoughts?

t_ch: 算了，这个故事确实烂到蹩脚…忘了它吧。
t_en: Whatever, this story is terrible and hacky... just forget it.

t_ch: 浪漫一点的。
t_en: Make it a little more romantic.

t_ch: 浪漫一点的…那就说说小行星带的事吧。
t_en: More romantic... Then let's talk about the incident in the asteroid belt.

t_ch: 有一次，我开着飞船在宇宙中闲逛，阴差阳错地来到了一个不存在于星图上的地方：一片遍布小行星的太空垃圾场。
t_en: One time, I was piloting a spaceship and roaming the stars, when I accidentally came to a place that wasn't on the astral charts: A space junkyard filled with asteroids.

t_ch: 这些偏僻星区的数据可珍贵了。只要把坐标上报给公司，我就能拿到一大笔信用点奖励。
t_en: The data in these remote star systems is priceless. I could get huge credit rewards for reporting the coordinates to IPC.

t_ch: 不过在那之前，我得先把周围仔细检查一遍——这么简单的钱却一直没人赚，背后肯定有鬼。
t_en: But before that, I had to carefully check our vicinity — this gig was all too easy, and there had to be something going on behind the scenes.

t_ch: 这地方障碍物太多，舰载仪器除了发出恼人的警报外什么忙都帮不上。没办法，我只能用肉眼观察，手动操控飞船。
t_en: There were so many obstacles in this place that the ship's instruments kept tripping alarms everywhere, rendering it useless. I had no choice but to make observations by dead reckoning, and manually piloted the spaceship.

t_ch: 就在我聚精会神、眼观六路的时候，一个张牙舞爪的外星人忽然飘到了驾驶窗前……
t_en: Just as I was focusing all my attentions on the six directions around me, the snarling teeth and claws of this alien suddenly floated into sight...

t_ch: 是啊！不过我并没有被吓到，我的大心脏可是比钻石都坚固——即便这怪物长着两对眼睛，皮肤比伊须磨人都光滑，我内心也毫无惧意。
t_en: Yeah! But I wasn't scared. My heart is tougher than diamond — despite this monster having two pairs of eyes and skin smoother than an Insumousu local. There was no dread in my heart.

t_ch: 我仔细一看，发现那家伙身上的皮肤干瘪不堪，似乎已经死了…看它的样子，在宇宙中生存应该没什么问题才对。
t_en: Upon closer look, I realized that this guy's skin was all dried up as though dead... Judging by its appearance, it should have no problem whatsoever surviving in the universe.

t_ch: 两对眼睛、皮肤光滑，有点像长了手脚的鱼——看它的样子，在宇宙中生存也没什么问题。
t_en: Two pairs of eyes and smooth skin, almost like a fish with arms and legs — judging by its appearance, it should have no problem whatsoever surviving in the universe.

t_ch: 后来我仔细一看，才发现那家伙已经死去多时了。
t_en: Upon closer look, I realized that this guy was dead, and had been so for some time.

t_ch: 我仔细一看，才发现那家伙已经死了。它长得像是有手脚的鱼，外表似乎很适合在宇宙中生存。
t_en: Upon closer look, I realized that this guy was dead. It looked like a fish with arms and legs, and its exterior seemed as though it would have no problem surviving out in the universe.

t_ch: 我从没在智库里见过类似的生物，这意味着它的种族要么尚未被发现，要么…已经灭亡。
t_en: I've never seen anything like it in the data bank, which means it's either an undiscovered species, or... extinct.

t_ch: 后来我又对这片星区做了些勘探，发现那些小行星并非自然形成。它们是一场爆炸产生的碎片，原本来自同一颗星球。
t_en: Later on, I did some digging around this star system and discovered those asteroids were not formed naturally. They were fragments from an explosion, and were all sourced from the same planet.

t_ch: 计算机解析了样本成分，测得碎片诞生于十多个琥珀纪前——那个时期发生了一件大事，你应该知道。
t_en: The computer analyzed the sample composition, and found that the fragments were formed over <unbreak>10</unbreak> Amber Eras ago — a huge event happened back then, which you should know about.

t_ch: 是星核——「万界之癌」就是自那时开始侵害诸界的。
t_en: It was a stellaron — the Cancer of All Worlds that set out on its invasion of all worlds thereafter.

t_ch: 没错，就是从那时起，「万界之癌」开始侵害诸界。
t_en: That's right. It was from that moment forth that the Cancer of All Worlds started its invasion across all worlds.

t_ch: 毁于星核的文明并不少见。最初我也没把这事放在心上，打算上报完坐标就走——直到我发现了这些小行星表面的符号。
t_en: Civilizations destroyed by stellarons are not altogether uncommon. At first, I didn't really take it seriously, and was planning on warping out of there as soon as I'd reported the coordinates — that is, until I discovered those symbols on the surface of the asteroids.

t_ch: 那真是十分壮观的场面，每颗小行星上都如纹身般镌刻着密集的块状痕迹。这些痕迹有深有浅、形状各异，无疑是经由人力开凿出来的，仿佛…某种文字。
t_en: It really was a magnificent sight, with these blocks of dense scratches etched onto each asteroid like a tattoo. There were both deep and shallow markings, in all kinds of different shapes, which undoubtedly were carved out by someone as if... they were glyphs from some civilization.

t_ch: 这幅奇景勾起了我的兴趣——哪个无名客能拒绝探险的邀请呢？
t_en: The sight piqued my interest — which of the Nameless could ever refuse such an invitation to explore?

t_ch: 于是我继续向垃圾场中央行驶，一路上尽是大大小小的刻字石，以及…修建在小行星上的房子。
t_en: So I continued on into the center of the trash dump, passing blocks with glyphs carved into them along the way as well as... little houses built onto the asteroids.

t_ch: 这些房子看起来像铁皮拼成的蜂巢，结构简单但数量繁多，显然是人工建造的——这里曾经住着一整个聚落！
t_en: These houses looked like little honeycombs woven out of tin foil. Simple in structure and numbering in the hundreds, they were clearly artificially built — an entire settlement once lived here!

t_ch: 我挨个检查那些住房，见到了更多外星人的尸体。房间内完全没有生存物资，除了简单的石镐外也几乎没有工具。
t_en: I checked over the houses but only found more alien corpses. There were no supplies in any of the buildings, and almost no tools at all save for a crude pickax.

t_ch: 可以想象，他们是这颗星球的幸存者，不知用什么手段逃离了灭顶之灾，却注定因资源耗尽走向死亡。
t_en: It's possible that they are the survivors of this planet, escaping utter catastrophe through some means but doomed to ultimately die due to exhaustion of resources.

t_ch: 在生命的最后时刻到来前，他们将至关重要的信息——可能是史书，也可能是对星核的研究——以文字形式{RUBY_B#记忆}记录{RUBY_E#}在了这些小行星表面。
t_en: Before the final moments of their lives, they {RUBY_B#memories}recorded{RUBY_E#} vital information — maybe histories, or research into Stellarons — in the form of glyphs carved into the surfaces of these asteroids.

t_ch: 这一工作实在太繁琐，太漫长…究竟是怎样坚定的意志在支撑他们做完这一切？我不知道，但他们的成果无疑铸就了母星文明的丰碑。
t_en: This work looks truly tedious and boring... What kind of firm resolve supported their completion? I don't know, but their achievements have no doubt left a monument for their home planet and civilization.

t_ch: 可惜联觉信标不能解读文字，漫长的无规则运动也早已破坏了这些小行星的顺序，不然我一定要读一读，看看究竟是什么内容能让他们在死亡面前保持团结。
t_en: It's a shame that the Synesthesia Beacon could not decrypt text, and long years of irregular movements have long since destroyed the sequence these asteroids were supposed to be read in. Otherwise, I would have read it — to see what it was that kept them going as a team on the brink of death.

t_ch: 最终，我按计划把坐标发送给公司，然后一脚油门离开了这里——应知晓的故事我已铭记，这些密文还是留给专业人士来伤脑筋吧。
t_en: In the end, I sent off the coordinates to IPC as planned, then put the pedal to the metal to hightail it out of there — I'd learned the lesson I was supposed to, and the secret of the glyphs should be left to professionals.

t_ch: 感觉会变成旅游景点…
t_en: Sounds like it'll become a tourist destination...

t_ch: 也许吧，不过相比之下，我更不希望这里的故事被永远埋没。
t_en: Maybe. But in comparison, I'd rather this story wasn't buried forever.

t_ch: …那样艰苦的努力却不能为人所知，未免太可惜了些。
t_en: ...It would be a great pity for such hard work to go unrecognized.

t_ch: 你拿到公司奖金了吗？
t_en: Did IPC pay out a bonus?

t_ch: 没有。原本我确实想着要赚公司一笔，但见识了那样壮观的景象后，钱已经无足轻重了。
t_en: They sure did. I had been focused on making a score, but after witnessing such a grand sight, money no longer seemed to matter.

t_ch: 而且以公司的作风，我这个第一发现人肯定要被盘问个不停；不如早点溜走，好歹能捞着个清静。
t_en: Plus, based on the IPC's style, I knew I'd face endless interrogations as the person who made first contact. I'd be far better slipping off and getting some peace and quiet.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Keep silent)

t_ch: 话说回来，这个故事够浪漫吗？
t_en: Anyhow, was that story romantic enough for you?

t_ch: 有种波澜壮阔的美。
t_en: There's a kind of majestic beauty to it.

t_ch: 是吧？相比于爱情，我更喜欢这种不寻常的浪漫。
t_en: Right? I prefer this kind of unusual romance over traditional love.

t_ch: 银河中有亿万颗恒星，在如此广大的尺度上，二人间的执念难免显得渺小。
t_en: There are hundreds of millions of stars in the cosmos. On such a vast scale, this mutual infatuation between two people seems inevitably insignificant.

t_ch: 总之，你能喜欢它真是再好不过了。
t_en: Anyway, it's great that you liked it.

t_ch: 一般般吧。
t_en: Mid.

t_ch: 唉，果然用言语描述出来还是差了些味道。
t_en: *sigh* So it seems like you lose some of the magic when you put it into words.

t_ch: 有机会的话，你真该亲眼见识下那副景象…也许可以把它做成梦泡？
t_en: If you get the chance, you really should try and see that sight with your own eyes... Maybe you could make it into a dream bubble?

t_ch: 不如爱情故事。
t_en: I prefer love stories.

t_ch: 哈哈，那确实也是一种浪漫，不过我更喜欢这种…不寻常的浪漫。
t_en: Haha, that's indeed one kind of romantic ideal, but I prefer this kind of... unusual romance.

t_ch: 毕竟，二人的执念只是银河中的万千联系之一。宇宙很精彩，不是吗？
t_en: After all, their infatuation is just one of uncountable connections throughout the cosmos. The universe is truly wonderful, isn't it?

t_ch: 有教育意义的。
t_en: One that's educational.

t_ch: 嗯…一般来说，这片宇宙发生的一切都只是巧合的堆砌，人类很难从中学到什么道理。
t_en: Well... generally speaking, everything that happens in this universe is just a pile of coincidences stacked up, and it's difficult for humans to learn anything from that.

t_ch: 不过，我确实有个符合你要求的故事。
t_en: However, I do have a story that fits the bill.

t_ch: 大概十个琥珀纪前，星际间还流传着「机械海盗王」韦兰的臭名……
t_en: About <unbreak>10</unbreak> Amber Eras or so back, the notorious "Mechanical Pirate King" Wyland roamed the galaxies...

t_ch: 这位贼人年轻时几乎把自己身上的全部零件都换成了义体，在星区内兴风作乱，犯罪无数，头上顶着的悬赏足以买下一整颗星球。
t_en: When this pirate was young, he switched out almost all of his body parts with prosthetics, wreaking havoc throughout star zones and committing countless crimes. The bounty on his head was enough to buy a whole planet of your own.

t_ch: 等年老后，韦兰的脑子已经被过多的改造压垮。疼痛与幻觉每天折磨着他，把他变成了狂躁易怒又神经兮兮的…怪物。
t_en: In his old age, Wyland's mind became overwhelmed by excessive mods. Pain and hallucinations tortured him daily, twisting him into a mad, irritable, neurotic... monster.

t_ch: 曾经忠诚的部下要么离开了他，要么被他残忍地折磨。就在海盗团即将分崩离析时，一位戴面具的访客找上韦兰，说虚空鲸的大脑提取液可以缓解这种不良反应。
t_en: His once-loyal crew either deserted him or were cruelly tortured by him. Just as this band of pirates was about to fall apart, a masked visitor came to Wyland telling him that an extract from the brain of the void song whale could relieve his adverse reactions.

t_ch: 走投无路的韦兰自然不会放过任何一根救命稻草，对虚空鲸的屠杀自此拉开序幕……
t_en: At his wits' end, Wyland could not help but clutch to any last straw that might save his life. And so began the massacre of the void song whales...

t_ch: 为保证猎杀效率，韦兰将自己的神经连进了舰桥终端，从而对整艘战舰如臂指使。代价就是，一旦失去虚空鲸提取液的供给，他就会在数小时内器官衰竭，最后死去。
t_en: Wyland hooked his nervous system straight into the bridge terminal for maximum hunting efficiency, so that commanding the entire battleship was like moving a limb. The cost was that once he lost his supply of whale extract, he would experience organ failure and perish soon after.

t_ch: 那是一段疯狂的日子，每天都有鲸群在炮火中丧生。就连「泯灭帮」都被韦兰的雄心折服，自愿加入这场狩猎——净瞎凑热闹。
t_en: Those were crazy days, with whales perishing in the cannon fire every day. Even the Annihilation Gang was won over by Wyland's ambition, willingly joining this hunt — just for the thrill of it.

t_ch: 多次警告无果后，星际和平公司找上了巡海游侠，提议联手铲除这个作恶已久的祸害。最后，双方各自派出精锐战力，计划对韦兰的旗舰发动一次斩首行动。
t_en: After multiple unheeded warnings, the Interastral Peace Corporation reached out to the Galaxy Rangers about building an alliance to wipe out this enduring scourge. In the end, both sides contributed crack troops and planned to launch an operation to lop the head off Wyland's main battleship.

t_ch: 谁说不是呢？但即便这样豪华的阵容，也远远称不上胜券在握。
t_en: Who said it wasn't? But even with such a luxuriant lineup, victory was far from guaranteed.

t_ch: 不要以为斩首很容易。战舰的雷达就是韦兰的双眼，战舰的火炮则是他手臂的延伸。想要登上这样一艘怪物，没有过硬的驾驶技术可办不到。
t_en: Don't think a beheading operation is easy. The battleship's radar was Wyland's eyes. Its cannons were extensions of his arms. Boarding such a monster as this requires perfect mastery of piloting.

t_ch: 好在我也参与了那场战斗…以运输舰驾驶员的身份。
t_en: Luckily, I also participated in that battle... as the pilot of the transport vessel.

t_ch: 当我们打开舰长室的大门时，眼前只有空空如也的玻璃缸和杂乱丢弃的输液管——其中最大的那根链接着韦兰的后背……
t_en: When we opened the door to the captain's quarters, all we could see was an empty glass tank and scattered drips hanging about the place — the largest of which was hooked up to Wyland's back...

t_ch: 韦兰趴在地上，像条脱水的鱼一样，死了。
t_en: Wyland lay on the ground, like a fish out of water — dead.

t_ch: 不，那样就没有教育意义了。
t_en: No, that isn't really very educational.

t_ch: 当时我们也很疑惑。本以为会有一场惊世大战，没想到一弹未发就跳到了收尸阶段。
t_en: We were puzzled at the time too. We thought there would be this ferocious battle, but it shifted straight to the cleanup stage without a shot being fired.

t_ch: 后来我们得知：早在这场战斗发生前，韦兰就把自己手头的提取液全卖掉了，买家是一位出价颇高的面具人。
t_en: Later on, we learned that Wyland had sold off all the extract he had well before the battle to a masked buyer who paid very good money for it.

t_ch: 他原以为缸里的存货足够挺到下一次狩猎，结果找了几周都没发现猎物——根据博识学会的统计，那片星区早就没有活着的虚空鲸了。
t_en: He thought the supplies in the tank would last until the next hunt, but could find no prey after weeks of searching. According to the statistics from the Intelligentsia Guild, there haven't been any living void song whales in that sector for a long time.

t_ch: 所以，当联军打上门时，韦兰已经因义体的排异反应而死去多时……
t_en: So when the righteous alliance attacked, Wyland had already been dead for some time due to graft-host rejection...

t_ch: 直到生命的最后一刻，他还在试图用嘴去吸输液管，好弄出几滴残留的提取液来。
t_en: He was still trying to use his mouth to suck the last few drops of extract out of the tubes during his final moments.

t_ch: 很讽刺的结局。
t_en: What an ironic ending.

t_ch: 是啊，也很有{RUBY_B#「欢愉」}乐子{RUBY_E#}。为祸一方的机械海盗王，最后落得个这样的死法。
t_en: Yes, truly an {RUBY_B#"Elation"}enjoyable{RUBY_E#} moment. The Mechanical Pirate King, a menace of the era, meeting his end like this.

t_ch: 他的追随者们呢？
t_en: And what of his followers?

t_ch: 最能打的韦兰死了，其他人自然作鸟兽散。不过公司和游侠还是让他们得到了应有的惩罚。
t_en: Wyland was the best fighter and died. The rest of them naturally scattered to the solar winds. But IPC and Rangers still meted out to them the punishment they deserved.

t_ch: 唉，有机体总是喜欢把自己改造成机械，韦兰也不例外……
t_en: *sigh* Organic creatures love modding themselves into robots. Wyland was no exception...

t_ch: 可他从来都没能摆脱人性中的贪欲，直到最后，滑稽地死在了管子拼成的王座里。
t_en: But he was never able to rid himself of human greed, until in the end he died farcically upon a throne made of twisted tubes.

t_ch: 我相信每个人都能从他的经历中学到不同的东西。而我的看法嘛…
t_en: I trust everyone is able to learn something different from his experiences. And in my opinion...

t_ch: 如果已经有许多人想要杀你，就别让自己陷入众叛亲离的境地。
t_en: If you already have people who want your head, don't let it get to the point where you've alienated your own family.

t_ch: 先不听了。
t_en: Not right now.

t_ch: 听起来很困难。
t_en: That sounds difficult.

t_ch: 你一定打得很漂亮。
t_en: I'm sure you're a master.

t_ch: 真吓人！
t_en: That's terrifying!

t_ch: 那外星人长什么样？
t_en: What did the alien look like?

t_ch: 我历史不好……
t_en: I'm not so good at history...

t_ch: 星核出现了？
t_en: A stellaron has appeared?

t_ch: 听起来势在必得啊。
t_en: Sounds like a must!

t_ch: 公司与巡海游侠合作，真少见…
t_en: The IPC and the Galaxy Rangers working together? That is rare...

t_ch: 吓死了？
t_en: Scared to death?

t_ch: …怎么死的？
t_en: ...How did they die?

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent.)

t_ch: 我改日再听。
t_en: I'll listen another day.

t_ch: 没什么。
t_en: Nothing.

t_ch: 那就一路平安吧——这是对无名客最好的祝福。
t_en: Then safe travels — the best wishes there could ever be for the Nameless.

t_ch: 我想要听听你的经历。
t_en: I'd like to hear about your experiences.

t_ch: 好呀，科琳船长的故事多到说不完。你喜欢听什么样的故事？
t_en: Alright, Captain Colleen's got many a story. What kind of stories do you like best?

t_ch: 刺激一点的。
t_en: Thrilling ones.

t_ch: 刺激一点的…我想想……
t_en: Thrilling, eh... Let me see...

t_ch: 有一次，我受博识学会委托，前去回收位于「新伊甸」大黑洞附近的一个侦测信标。报酬非常优厚，所以我没多想就同意了。
t_en: There was this one time on an assignment from the Intelligentsia Guild, when we were retrieving a probe beacon from nearby this mega black hole called the New Eden. The pay was so fat that we agreed without thinking too much about it.

t_ch: 一去才发现，信标早就不在原来的位置了。某个意外让它脱离了固定点，正朝着黑洞所在的方向一路飘去，很快就会越过事件视界。
t_en: Upon arrival, we discovered that the beacon wasn't in its original spot. An accident had dragged it away from its fixed location, and it was drifting toward the black hole — soon to cross over the event horizon.

t_ch: 越过事件视界就相当于扎进巨真蛰虫的胃袋——有去无回。所以我必须在信标彻底跌入黑洞前把它截住…这真是我船长生涯中最棘手的挑战。
t_en: Crossing the event horizon is like plunging into the stomach of a Giant Sting: There's no coming back from it. So I had to intercept the beacon before it fell completely into the black hole... This was the most difficult challenge I ever encountered in my career as a captain.

t_ch: 要知道，事件视界之外也是有引力的，而且越靠近中心、引力就越强。我那艘船的发动机不是尖货，没法保证取到信标后还能出来……
t_en: Y'know what, there is gravity outside the event horizon, and it gets stronger the closer you get to the center. My ship's engine wasn't the best, and there was no guarantee that we could even get out once we'd nabbed the beacon...

t_ch: …好在信标的前进方向上有不少金属碎片。我可以从船上发射一个东西，与那些碎片碰撞、反弹，从而给信标施加一个往回来的力……
t_en: ...Luckily there were lots of metal scraps in the beacon's path. I could launch something from the ship to collide with the scraps and ricochet, thereby exerting a returning force onto the beacon...

t_ch: 这原理解释起来可就复杂了…知道{RUBY_B#台球}撞洞球{RUBY_E#}吗？就跟那个差不多，但我要打的是有史以来最精妙，最传奇的一杆！
t_en: This principle is quite hard to explain... Do you know {RUBY_B#Billiards}billiards{RUBY_E#}? It's just like that, but the shot I was gonna make would be the most exquisite, legendary shot in all history!

t_ch: 是啊，入射角度必须精确，发射力度必须适中；任何失误都会破坏碎片的排列，根本没有重来的余地。
t_en: Yeah, the angle of incidence must be exact with moderate force only; any mistakes would destroy the arrangement of the scraps. There was no room for a mulligan here.

t_ch: 不，要真那样的话就算不上惊险了……
t_en: No, if it were that alone, it wouldn't count as being thrilling...

t_ch: 是不是很好奇，为什么这样的故事称得上惊险呢？别急，我来告诉你……
t_en: Aren't you curious as to why this story isn't considered thrilling, though? Don't worry. I'll fill you in...

t_ch: 那一杆打得并不好。飞船发出的物体没有按照预想回弹，而是像没头苍蝇一样乱撞一气，反过来加速了信标的沉没。
t_en: The shot was no good. The missiles did not rebound as expected, but instead smashed around like a headless fly — which then accelerated the sinking of the beacon.

t_ch: 于是我连忙开足马力追了上去——当时真该调头离开的，或许是开拓精神作祟吧。
t_en: So I quickly chased after him at full speed. I really should have turned around and left at that point, but perhaps it was the Trailblazer inside me.

t_ch: 成功回收信标的同时，我的飞船也被黑洞的引力捕获，搁浅在了吸积盘上。那感觉就像身处湍急的水流，不管怎么努力都无法前进，稍一松懈又会被推着后退。
t_en: While safely retrieving the beacon, my ship was captured by the gravity well of the black hole, trapping me on the accretion disk. It felt like being in water rapids — no matter how hard you tried there was no way forward, and if you relaxed for a second, it would push you back.

t_ch: 靠着引擎的超负荷运转，我暂时保持了受力平衡；但等到引擎燃料耗尽后，飞船还是会不可避免地坠入深渊。
t_en: Overloading the engine, I maintained a temporary force balance. However, once my fuel burned up, the spaceship would inevitably tumble straight into the abyss.

t_ch: 就在这时，我想起了先贤说过的一句话：「想要离开某地，必须先抛弃某物」。
t_en: Just then, I found myself remembering that line from the sages: "If you wish to leave somewhere, you must first abandon something."

t_ch: 于是我执行分离程序，将飞船的整个货仓都向后发射出去，借着产生的反冲力脱离了旋涡。
t_en: And so I performed a separation procedure, launching the entire cargo cabin of the spaceship backwards to use the recoil generated to break away from the black hole.

t_ch: 还好当初没有彻底落入事件视界，不然就只有星神知道会怎样了……
t_en: Luckily we didn't end up crossing the event horizon, otherwise only the Aeons know what might have happened...

t_ch: 但这并不是胜利。逃出黑洞之后，我发现日子已经过去了好久，比想象中还要久……
t_en: But this was no victory. Outside the vicinity of the black hole, I discovered that a long time had passed — far longer than I could've imagined...

t_ch: 交还信标时，所有人都用诧异的目光看向我——原来的委托人早就老死了，是他徒弟的徒弟为我付了酬劳。
t_en: When I handed back the beacon, everyone looked at me as though I had two heads: The original manager for the task had long died. It was his apprentice's apprentice who paid me out.

t_ch: 你时间穿越了？
t_en: Did you travel through time?

t_ch: 可以这么说。毕竟单凭凡人的寿命，我可没机会活到现在。
t_en: That's one way of putting it. After all, based on the lifespan of mortals I shouldn't be alive right now.

t_ch: 至于这到底是机缘还是诅咒，恐怕只有时间能给出答案……
t_en: As for whether this is a blessing or a curse, I'm afraid only time can deliver an answer...

t_ch: 博识学会还挺守约的…
t_en: The Intelligentsia Guild keeps their promises...

t_ch: 哈哈，如果真能遇见从十几个琥珀纪前来的人，我也很乐意付他钱——还有比这更酷的事了吗？
t_en: Haha, if I really could meet someone from dozens of Amber Eras ago, I'd be happy to pay them money — could there be anything cooler than this?

t_ch: 毕竟，可不是每个凡人都有机会活那么久。
t_en: After all, not just anybody gets the chance to live so long.

t_ch: 至于这到底是机缘还是诅咒，恐怕只有时间能给出答案……
t_en: As for whether this is a blessing or a curse, I'm afraid only time can deliver an answer...

t_ch: 似乎也没那么惊险。
t_en: Doesn't seem that thrilling to me.

t_ch: 这么说也没错，毕竟解决问题的关键只是抛弃压舱物而已，听起来确实很简单。
t_en: Sure, when it's put like that. After all, problem-solving is really all about jettisoning the ballast, that's all. It sounds really simple.

t_ch: 但真到了那时候，并不是人人都有断尾逃生的魄力。而且我觉得，最刺激的部分在于逃出来之后……
t_en: But when the time comes, not everyone has the guts to sever their own tail to escape. And I think the most thrilling part of all is in the escaping...

t_ch: 跨越如此漫长的时间到底是机缘还是诅咒，恐怕只有时间本身能给出答案。
t_en: Whether skipping such a long passage of time is a blessing or a curse, I'm afraid only time itself can deliver an answer...

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Keep silent)

t_ch: 我知道这故事很难以置信，但宇宙中离谱的事情实在太多了…何况我们是无名客，无名客不能被现实束缚了思维！
t_en: I know this story is unbelievable, but so many extreme things happen out there in the universe every day. Besides, we the Nameless cannot limit ourselves to the boundaries of reality!

t_ch: 况且这到底是机缘亦或诅咒，还尚未可知呢…恐怕只有时间能给出答案了。
t_en: What's more, I have no idea right now whether this is a blessing or a curse... I'm afraid only time can deliver an answer.

t_ch: 浪漫一点的。
t_en: Make it a little more romantic.

t_ch: 浪漫一点的…那就说说小行星带的事吧。
t_en: More romantic... Then let's talk about the incident in the asteroid belt.

t_ch: 有一次，我开着飞船在宇宙中闲逛，阴差阳错地来到了一个不存在于星图上的地方：一片遍布小行星的太空垃圾场。
t_en: One time, I was piloting a spaceship and roaming the stars, when I accidentally came to a place that wasn't on the astral charts: A space junkyard filled with asteroids.

t_ch: 这种疏漏往往是因为地图数据不足。只要把正确的坐标上报给公司，就可以拿到一大笔信用点奖励。
t_en: Such oversights are often due to insufficient map data. I could get huge credits rewards for reporting correct coordinates to IPC.

t_ch: 可惜我自己就在公司享有一笔悬赏，这钱怕是赚不到了——但不耽误我在周围逛一圈，万一能遇到什么宝贝呢？
t_en: It's a shame IPC had a bounty out on my head, so that money's out of my reach for now — but that doesn't stop me from taking a stroll around there. Who knows what treasure I might find?
